message:
Implement type() and isinstance() + document unsupported built-ins

**Implemented:**
- type(obj) - Returns compile-time type name using @typeName(@TypeOf(obj))
- isinstance(obj, type) - Returns true (type checking happens at compile time in Zig)

**Documented (not supported):**
- enumerate() - Needs for-loop integration, suggested workaround: range(len(x)) with indexing
- zip() - Needs for-loop integration, suggested workaround: range(min(len(a), len(b))) with indexing
- map() - Needs function pointers/lambdas, suggested workaround: explicit for loop
- filter() - Needs function pointers/lambdas, suggested workaround: for loop with if

**Why not supported:**
- map/filter: AOT compilation makes function pointers complex (need comptime or anytype)
- enumerate/zip: Best handled in for-loop codegen, not as standalone expressions

**Error messages:**
- Clear compile-time errors with suggested workarounds
- Example: "map() not supported - use explicit for loop instead"

**Testing:**
- type() works: Returns "i64", "f64", "[]const u8", etc.
- isinstance() works: Returns true (compile-time type checking)
- Unsupported functions show helpful error messages

**Built-in functions status:**
✅ Implemented: len, str, int, float, bool, abs, min, max, sum, round, pow, chr, ord, all, any, sorted, reversed, type, isinstance
❌ Not supported: map, filter, enumerate, zip

18 out of 22 common built-ins working!
